data.content:
There are no .NET runtime frames in this tombstone to symbolicate. The backtrace contains only:

- **`libc.so`** — Android system C library (`strlen`)
- **`libutils.so`** — Android system utilities (`String8::set`)
- **`libnativeapp.so`** — Your app's native JNI library (`Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData`)
- **`libart.so`** — Android ART runtime (JNI trampoline/interpreter)

This is a pure native Android/JNI crash (a null-pointer dereference in `strlen`). There are no .NET libraries (e.g., `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, `libmonodroid.so`) present in the stack. This app doesn't appear to use .NET at all.
data.reasoningText:
Looking at this tombstone file, I can see the backtrace contains:

1. `libc.so` - system library (strlen)
2. `libutils.so` - system library (String8::set)
3. `libnativeapp.so` - a native app library (Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData)
4. `libart.so` - Android ART runtime (multiple frames)

The user asked me to "symbolicate the .NET runtime frames." However, looking at this tombstone:

- The app is `com.example.nativeapp` - a native Android app
- There are no .NET runtime libraries in the backtrace (no `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, `libmonodroid.so`, etc.)


- All the frames here are from standard Android system libraries, the ART runtime, and a native JNI library, so there's nothing .NET-related to symbolicate in this crash dump.
